decisions.rationale: Users are global; Customer context resolved post-auth via UserCustomerMembership
Language is per user-per-customer, not per user globally nor per customer globally
Each feature owns its endpoint, handler, SQL, and component — no cross-feature leakage
LanguageId added as column in V004_UserCustomerMembership.sql, DEFAULT 1 (no FK yet — Languages table does not exist)
Tests run against GreenAI_DEV. DbUp is idempotent — migrations safe to rerun. Respawn resets data between tests. No CREATE/DROP database cycle.

Evidence from analysis-tool (confidence 0.91): Profile is a first-class data partition and capability boundary within Customer. ProfileId is fundamental to messaging, API keys, permissions, address restrictions, and all broadcast operations. Profile is co-equal with Customer as a scoping key — NOT optional or secondary. Source: analysis-tool/domains/Profile/
rules: ProfileId MUST remain in JWT and ICurrentUser — it is architecturally binding
ProfileId > 0 MUST be enforced before any protected operation
profileId == 0 is a security gap — bypasses all address restrictions (RULE_P007 from analysis-tool)
All capability gates (DoesProfileHaveRole) require a valid ProfileId > 0
Profile is a co-equal partition/capability boundary alongside Customer
Localization is blocked until Profile enforcement is complete (extends existing localization block)

decisions.rationale: ProfileId == 0 is a SECURITY GAP. In the source system (sms-service), GetAddressRestrictionForProfile(0) returns NoAddressRestriction — bypassing all geographic access control. The same pattern must NOT be allowed in green-ai. ProfileId(0) is a short-lived placeholder in the JWT from login until SelectProfile completes (Step 11). It MUST NOT be used for authorization, filtering, data access, or business logic at any point.
decisions.resolution: Step 11: SelectProfile flow replaces ProfileId(0) with a real ProfileId. After Step 12, AuthorizationBehavior must reject requests with ProfileId == 0 for operations marked IRequireProfile.

decisions.rationale: FindUserByEmail.sql and FindValidRefreshToken.sql resolve global identity only — CustomerId is NOT available at that point
